host_model_constraints: NGINX request and response body filters feed bounded body data into a completed ModSecurity phase; they do not provide streaming phase semantics.
The connector JSONL writer is currently specific to Phase-4 interventions and does not by itself prove canonical event coverage for request-phase decisions.
A Phase-4 decision can be observed after NGINX has sent response headers and is then governed by the late-intervention policy.

connection_metadata.reason: The native request path maps NGINX connection endpoints into the ModSecurity transaction; canonical evidence is pending.

request_headers.reason: The access handler maps NGINX request headers and invokes request-header processing; canonical evidence is pending.

request_body_buffered.reason: The request-body callback appends buffered NGINX body buffers and completes Phase 2; canonical body evidence is pending.

request_body_streaming.reason: Body buffers are accumulated for a completed request-body phase rather than exposed as streaming ModSecurity phase execution.

response_headers.reason: The NGINX header filter invokes response-header processing; canonical Phase-3 evidence is pending.

response_body_buffered.reason: The bounded body filter invokes response-body processing and applies the configured Phase-4 policy; canonical evidence is pending.

response_body_streaming.reason: Response chains are inspected through bounded buffering and do not expose streaming Phase-4 semantics.

phase1.reason: The access handler processes URI and request headers before upstream handling; canonical Phase-1 evidence is pending.

phase2.reason: The request-body callback completes ModSecurity request-body processing; canonical Phase-2 evidence is pending.

phase3.reason: The response header filter invokes ModSecurity Phase 3; canonical behavioral evidence is pending.

phase4.reason: The response body filter invokes bounded ModSecurity Phase 4; canonical behavioral evidence is pending.

phase4_rule_evaluation.reason: The bounded native response-body filter is wired to invoke Phase 4, but no current canonical real-host run proves that Phase-4 rule 1100301 was evaluated.

phase4_pre_commit_deny.reason: The native Phase-4 decision runs in the NGINX body filter after the response-header path; this connector has no demonstrated pre-commit response-body decision point, so a visible Phase-4 HTTP 403 must not be claimed.

late_intervention.reason: The body filter distinguishes pre-header denial from post-header log-only or connection-abort policy outcomes; canonical evidence is pending.

late_intervention_log_only.reason: The safe post-commit policy is wired to record a log-only outcome, but no canonical event proves requested deny, actual log_only, late_intervention=true, and an unchanged visible status.

late_intervention_abort.reason: The strict post-commit policy has an abort path, but no canonical real-host event proves actual abort_connection and connection_aborted=true.

late_intervention_status_metadata.reason: The Phase-4 path can emit intervention metadata, but no canonical event yet proves separate requested WAF status, original host status, visible client status, requested action, and actual action.

deny.reason: Disruptive statuses are returned from access and filter phases; canonical deny evidence is pending.

redirect.reason: The intervention mapper creates an NGINX Location header and returns redirect status before headers are sent; canonical evidence is pending.

drop.reason: The NGINX intervention mapper does not preserve a distinct ModSecurity drop action.

abort_connection.reason: The strict Phase-4 branch has an explicit connection-abort outcome after headers are sent; transport evidence is pending.

log_only.reason: Non-disruptive rules pass and late Phase-4 policy records log-only outcomes; canonical evidence is pending.

transaction_id.reason: NGINX complex-value transaction IDs are used when creating transactions; canonical event correlation evidence is pending.

event_jsonl.reason: The Phase-4 filter serializes bounded common JSONL events, but request-phase canonical event coverage is not yet asserted.

config_inline_rules.reason: The modsecurity_rules directive loads inline rules through libmodsecurity; canonical config evidence is pending.

config_rules_file.reason: The modsecurity_rules_file directive loads local rules files; canonical positive and negative evidence is pending.

config_remote_rules.reason: The modsecurity_rules_remote directive is wired to libmodsecurity; the no-CRS baseline does not exercise external networking.

transport_metadata.reason: The native filters retain response status and commitment state, but no canonical transport run has recorded it.

request_body_incremental_ingest.reason: The native request-body callback begins after NGINX has collected the host request body; it is not a real incremental host-ingestion path.

response_body_incremental_ingest.reason: The body filter appends each current NGINX buffer without retaining a cross-call response body, but no native-host evidence run is attached.

phase4_end_of_stream_evaluation.reason: The body filter finalizes response-body processing once at last_buf or last_in_chain; canonical host evidence is pending.

content_type_scope.reason: The Phase-4 filter checks its configured response Content-Type scope before appending body bytes; real-host coverage is pending.

header_limits.reason: No canonical NGINX connector path asserts the shared header-limit behavior in the full-lifecycle catalog.

request_body_limits.reason: The current request-body path is host-buffered and has no connector-level incremental limit outcome contract.

response_body_limits.reason: The response filter bounds bytes supplied to ModSecurity with the configured Phase-4 limit, but limit-mode evidence is pending.

no_full_response_buffering.reason: The response filter processes and forwards current chain buffers rather than accumulating a connector-owned full response; synchronized proof is pending.

first_byte_before_response_end.reason: The pass-through filter wiring permits downstream delivery before end of stream, but the synchronized first-byte test has not run.

http1_content_length.reason: The native NGINX module can run behind HTTP/1.1 Content-Length responses, but the canonical transport case has not run.

http1_chunked.reason: The body-filter path can receive HTTP/1.1 chunked response buffers, but no canonical transport result is attached.

keep_alive.reason: No canonical sequential-request keep-alive run is attached to the native module.

parallel_requests.reason: The canonical parallel full-lifecycle isolation evidence path is not implemented for this connector.

http2.reason: The module can be built into an HTTP/2-capable NGINX host, but the currently pinned canonical host was configured without --with-http_v2_module. No applicable HTTP/2 lifecycle case is attached.

client_abort.reason: No native NGINX client-abort cleanup case currently records the canonical transport metadata.

upstream_abort.reason: No native NGINX upstream-abort cleanup case currently records the canonical transport metadata.

response_body_decompression.reason: The connector has no asserted response-body decompression contract.

source_contract.reason: Native source, metadata, filter wiring, and harness wiring are present; no canonical source-contract result is attached.

compile.reason: A source-built NGINX module path exists; no current canonical compile result is attached.

link.reason: The build path links NGINX with the module, libmodsecurity, and common sources; no canonical link result is attached.

config_load.reason: The harness has a real nginx -t path; no current canonical result is attached.

start_smoke.reason: The harness can start NGINX without a request; no current canonical result is attached.

minimal_runtime_smoke.reason: A real-host request harness exists; no current canonical minimal-runtime result is attached.

no_crs_baseline.reason: The native host path can consume the shared no-CRS rules and cases, but no canonical baseline result is attached.

crs_smoke.reason: Legacy limited CRS smoke wiring exists and is not baseline evidence.

extended_matrix.reason: Extended-matrix verification is outside this canonical no-CRS declaration.
